items.correct: Learning by observing the consequences others receive for behaviour.
items.misconception: Being directly rewarded for copying someone.
items.why: Students confuse vicarious with direct reinforcement. 'Vicarious' means experienced through someone else - you see them rewarded/punished and adjust your own behaviour accordingly.

items.q: What does a correlation of -0.8 indicate?
items.misconception: A weak relationship (because it is negative).
items.why: Students treat the negative sign as 'weak'. The sign shows DIRECTION (as one variable goes up, the other goes down); the absolute value shows STRENGTH. -0.8 is strong.

items.q: In Milgram's study, what percentage of participants gave the maximum 450V shock?
items.why: Students remember 'most' obeyed and assume all did. Actually 65% went to 450V; 35% defied. This nuance matters because it shows obedience is strong but not absolute.

items.q: Top-down offender profiling was developed by:
items.why: Correct answer: FBI. “Canter” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Differential association theory was proposed by:
items.why: Correct answer: Sutherland. “Eysenck” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Lombroso publish his atavistic form theory?
items.why: Exact answer: 1876 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
